message:
fix(security): second-pass audit fixes — object key handling, error variants, Azure encoding

Three categories of bugs found and fixed during second-pass code audit:

1. **S3/GCS/KV object keys need forward slashes**: sanitize_path_segment()
   was rejecting valid object keys like "photos/2024/sunset.jpg". Added
   sanitize_object_key() (AWS), sanitize_object_name() (GCP), and
   sanitize_kv_key() (Cloudflare) that allow `/` but still block `..`
   and `\` for path traversal prevention.

2. **Wrong error variant in sanitize functions**: 5 connectors used
   ::Api error variant for input validation (maps to FcpError::External),
   fixed to use ::InvalidInput (maps to FcpError::InvalidRequest).
   Affected: Cloudflare, AWS, GCP, Netlify, DockerHub, PayPal.

3. **Azure NON_ALPHANUMERIC encoding too aggressive**: percent_encoding
   with NON_ALPHANUMERIC encoded `-`, `_`, `.` in container/blob names,
   breaking valid Azure paths. Replaced with custom AZURE_PATH_SAFE set
   that only encodes truly unsafe characters (controls, space, #, ?, etc.)

All 649 tests pass across 10 connectors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
